Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Qiu Yun King To Further Respond To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Her Attorneys Of Record In The Sum Of $9,060
Off Calendar Jun-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WILLIAM E. WEISS, Esq. of Law Offices of William e. Weiss (Tel:415-362-6765) for Plaintiffs
Atty. LINDA HWEE, Esq. of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth (Tel:650-592-5400) for defendants QUI YUN KING & DARRYL DA YUN QUI,

The Court's tentative ruling was not timely contested. As such the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem hearing this motion.

Ms. Hwee attempted to present oral argument. She claimed an assistant at her firm timely emailed notice of contest of the tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem Peter Vestal declined to accept arguments of counsel as the Court never received notice of contest from anybody in the subject matter. Court advised counsel of its intent to confirm the tenative ruling.

Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HAN CHEN'S Motion To Compel Defendant Qiu Yun King To Further Respond To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Her Attorneys Of Record In The Sum Of $9,060

The motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Plaintiff's counsel is to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in word format and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Peter Vestal at PVestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
